text: >> Oh, hello? >> Hello. >> There was no car in the garage, but we probably lost. >> Hello. >> Good afternoon. My name is Cassie. So, I'm calling because your Medicare member profile hasn't been transferred into Medicare's national database for 2026 yet, and that can hold up your coverage approvals. So, you do have both part A and part B, right? >> Yes, you keep calling me the same question. >> That might have been someone else. This is my first time calling. I know it's frustrating, and I'm sorry, I'm calling about 2026 Medicare benefits, including food, flex cards, and cash back up to $250. So, you do have both part A and part B of Medicare, right? >> Yes, because of whom you are you calling? >> So, being covered is one thing. Staying up to date is another. The new 2026 benefits are here, and I'm here to help you get the best plan. So, how old are you? >> I'm 91, and I'm not qualified. >> For your age, you're eligible for the benefits we have for multiple providers. Our supervisors on the line connecting you now, okay? >> All right, the age assistance test. >> You're almost there. Let me connect you with our supervisor, okay? >> Okay. >> Just a few seconds, please hold. >> Hi, thank you for holding on the line. My name is Brian, how are you? >> All right, okay. Well, this call is in regards to your Medicare additional benefits and savings. I understand you have Medicare A and B, is that right? >> Yes, I have. >> All right, you know, we are in the end of the month. Just few days left in Medicare, allow every person to review or get extra benefits that you are entitled to, and that's the way you can save money. Do you pay any kind of co-pays regarding doctors and medications for this year? >> I don't know. >> You don't know about that? No co-pays, okay, no problem. And currently, do you have any chronic health conditions, diabetes, heart problems or any other? >> Yes, I'm a diabetic. >> Diabetic, okay, it's better to hear it. And do you have any food allowance card, like a benefits card from Medicare? Anything like that? >> No, no. >> You know, a lot of people are receiving. And you already know the situation, like everything is like expensive, right? Due to inflation, like almost everything is so high. People are getting like 120 or more than $150 per dollar allowance every month. So we just need to check how much maximum you can get, okay? How much more you could qualify. I just want to make sure few basic things that I will help you. And how old are you right now? >> 91. >> And do you make your own health care decision? >> Sometimes. [BLANK_AUDIO]
